visualGoal: Make a home builder confidently choose DIY vs premium enclosure and the right screen tier before they read the bounceback and aspect-ratio caveats. Real enclosure/screen/projector/mat product proof throughout; no procedural art. Hero band left untouched.

heroImage.notes: Existing topic hero at public/images/golf/golf-simulator-impact-screen-enclosure.jpg. Left as-is per hero-band lock. Not a /images/gear/ product photo, so not in verified-product-images.json — it is the article hero, not a product embed.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic dedicated home golf bay at controlled light, a taut impact screen glowing with a sunlit links fairway inside a deep-green padded enclosure, open overlay space at lower-left for a real enclosure/screen product photo, curator Nina Brooks gesturing at the air gap behind the screen, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, warped screen, impossible geometry, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: Bounceback is an install problem
content: Leave an air gap behind the screen
Result: ball drops 1-3 ft, not back at you
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable text, golf greens, real screen/enclosure photos as row markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Aspect ratio + projector throw = one decision
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered comparison, projector-photo placeholders for Codex overlays

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult golf curator Nina Brooks in a clean home golf bay, one hand behind a taut impact screen showing the air gap to the wall, confident instructor energy, product overlay space beside her, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

openQuestions: Confirm current Carl's DIY kit price (promo-dependent, ~$749.95 sale to ~$999.95 regular)?
Confirm current SIG10 price (Shop Indoor Golf $1,999.99; other retailers to ~$2,499)?
sig10-enclosure product JSON says 'steel-pipe framing' but the live retailer page says 'color-coded push-pin poles' — reconcile the JSON in a future data pass (no image change needed).
